content.claim: Tracking via gimnázium selection can improve short-term achievement of selected students while producing deteriorating test scores in non-selected cohorts, with long-run impact that disappears over time
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Single-study finding not universally replicated in Hungarian context
Short-term improvement does not guarantee long-term educational or labor-market benefit
Does not control for confounding selection mechanisms or residential segregation
Academic framing differs between causal claims and observed correlations
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
